query: Review an existing breadboard against the implementation. First sync the breadboard so it matches the code, then identify design problems and suggest boundary or naming fixes.
expected_behavior: Performs the work in two phases: sync to implementation first, then reflect on design quality
Treats the code as ground truth instead of assuming the breadboard is correct
Identifies at least one concrete design issue such as hidden stores, naming resistance, or boundary problems
Suggests breadboard fixes that make the behavior easier to explain
